title: ‘It’s Russian roulette’: alarm as Europe backs critical minerals mines in water-stressed regions
summary: The EU Commission is moving to relax water protection regulations to accelerate critical minerals extraction in drought-prone regions, creating regulatory and operational risks for infrastructure investors in mining and water assets. This policy shift signals both opportunity in critical minerals infrastructure but substantial ESG and stranded asset risks, particularly for water utilities and environmental remediation services in affected regions.

title: Disruption Matters: The Exit Playbook
summary: Private equity managers are adapting exit strategies to navigate a challenging macro environment with compressed IRR expectations and limited traditional exit routes. The focus on profitable realizations amidst market turbulence suggests evolving playbooks for infrastructure asset monetization and portfolio optimization.

title: Skyborn Renewables to sell 25% stake in German offshore wind farm
summary: Skyborn Renewables is divesting a 25% stake in a German offshore wind asset, likely signaling portfolio optimization or capital recycling in a maturing market. This transaction reflects ongoing consolidation in European offshore wind and presents potential entry/acquisition opportunities for infrastructure funds seeking de-risked renewable assets.

title: Sany commissions Spain's largest Chinese wind turbine
summary: Sany's deployment of large-scale wind turbines in Spain signals competitive pressure from Chinese manufacturers in European renewable energy infrastructure, a key sector for PE returns. This highlights consolidation opportunities and potential valuation impacts across European wind asset portfolios and OEM partnerships.

title: The so-called ‘zombie fund’ problem persists and LPs are concerned
summary: Zombie funds—vehicles with extended tail-end periods holding legacy assets—are expected to increase as liquidity pressures and GP differentiation challenges persist, constraining capital deployment and exit timing for European infrastructure investors. This trend may compress valuations and create competitive pressure on newer funds seeking to deploy capital into quality infrastructure assets.
pe_angle: Fund lifecycle and capital deployment efficiency
